pages.title: Inscribed terrain
pages.text: $(l)山$() and $(l)裂$() both use the inscription circle. Press $(l)V$(), choose a world position, then write the glyph to reshape nearby terrain.$(br2)Both are temporary. They roll back on expiry, death, logout, or destruction, so they do not permanently pollute the save.

pages.title: 山: temporary cover
pages.text: $(l)山$() raises a 5-block-wide wall at the target. Its direction follows the way you were facing.$(br2)Wood makes a low ridge. Iron and above blocks mobs properly. Diamond and netherite add thorns that hurt enemies touching the wall. Gold makes the top glow.

pages.title: 裂: collapse trap
pages.text: $(l)裂$() triggers 1 second after writing, collapsing the target ground into a rift. Low tiers make a 3x3 pit, 2 blocks deep; gold and above expands to 5x5.$(br2)Falling in costs 1 heart. Monsters are lightly pushed away from the rim, and netherite rifts slow entities near the edge.

pages.title: Protection rules
pages.text: 山 and 裂 will not overwrite or destroy key blocks such as containers, redstone cores, portals, bedrock, obsidian, or command blocks.$(br2)If the target area is not safe to rewrite, the glyph fails with a message instead.
